description: A body or material 'kesiri' when its overall size or volume becomes smaller. Cloth may shrink after washing, and a swelling may shrink as fluid leaves. 'peki' (squeeze) concerns applied inward pressure; shrinking is the dimensional result whether or not pressure caused it.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Bring the tongue blade close to the alveolar ridge for unvoiced 's' and continue into stressed high-front unrounded 'i'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue tip at the alveolar ridge and finish with another high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: The opening velar stop gives way to two high-front 'i' vowels and a quick final tap. The mouth stays comparatively narrow through the stressed and final syllables, a compact contour beside the broader middle of 'murase' (swell).
usage_notes: 'kesiri' usually appears without an object. Causative 'ka' introduces what makes something shrink, and the event noun is shrinkage. Drying or cooling can be stated separately, as can any other cause.

semantic_domains.physical: decrease in bodily or material extent
semantic_domains.quantity: an overall size or volume becoming smaller
